behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is LLM correctness—whether the real model respects the specified interface. Mocking the LLM to always return a correct signature hides real failures: parameter name mismatches, missing type hints, invalid Python syntax. Only calling the real LLM exposes whether it reliably generates signatures matching the input specification.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM with a detailed description specifying exact parameter names (e.g., 'user_id', 'timestamp'), types (int, str, datetime), and return type. Parse the generated code using ast.parse(). Extract the function signature and verify parameter names, types, and return type match the description specification exactly.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration-level test with mocked LLM would only verify our parsing logic, not LLM interface compliance. Agentic-level test is necessary.

behaviors.description: The generated implementation passes all provided test cases on the first or a subsequent attempt
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires 'subsequent attempt'—the refinement loop. Real failure: 'code never passes tests even after 3 refinement cycles.' Requires real LLM (which may fail first attempt but succeed after receiving error feedback) and real test execution. Mocking either LLM or test framework hides whether refinement actually improves code quality.
behaviors.test_description: For a function that raw LLM calls typically fail on (multi-step algorithm, complex control flow), execute the full service loop: generate code → run test suite → if tests fail, include error messages in next prompt and regenerate → repeat up to 3 times. Verify the final code passes all test cases. Run multiple times to account for LLM non-determinism.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Single Agentic test (one LLM call) would only catch first-attempt success and miss refinement loop effectiveness. Must test full workflow.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement step includes the failure messages in the next LLM prompt so the model can learn from its mistakes
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is prompt construction and passing, not LLM correctness. Real failure: failure messages are dropped, truncated, malformed, or missing context in the prompt. Can mock the LLM and inspect call arguments to verify error messages are present; LLM's response is irrelevant. Real test execution is needed to generate authentic failure messages.
behaviors.test_description: Run a test case that fails (e.g., AssertionError or timeout). Capture the actual failure output. Mock the LLM client. Execute the refinement step. Inspect the mocked LLM call's prompt argument and verify: (1) failure message is present in full, (2) message is properly escaped for prompt injection, (3) context (line number, traceback) is preserved, (4) message is not truncated.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Could test at Agentic with real LLM, but that's unnecessary—correctness of prompt construction is independent of the LLM's output. Integration level is sufficient.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results
behaviors.justification: Pure control flow and data collection logic. Real failures: loop doesn't stop after attempt 3, attempt data is lost mid-loop, error structure is missing fields. These are logic failures independent of LLM or test correctness. Integration test with mocked LLM/test runner that fails predictably allows verification of loop termination and complete attempt collection.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the LLM to always return invalid code ('syntax error'). Run the service end-to-end. Verify: (1) exactly 3 LLM calls are made, no 4th attempt, (2) returned error object contains an 'attempts' list with 3 entries, (3) each entry has required fields: attempt_number, generated_code, test_output, error_message, (4) error.attempts[2].test_output matches the final test execution.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Could unit-test the counter logic in isolation, but that misses failures in the loop's data collection and early exit. Integration level catches the full control flow.

behaviors.description: The full loop—generate, test, refine, test again—produces a working implementation for a function that a single LLM call typically gets wrong
behaviors.justification: Explicitly tests 'full loop' and end-to-end refinement. 'Function that a single LLM call typically gets wrong' means only the real LLM fails, receives feedback, and improves—mocking destroys this. Real failure: refinement doesn't help; code never passes despite multiple attempts. Requires real LLM non-determinism (may fail attempt 1, succeed attempt 2), real error messages, and multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Select a function known to require multi-step refinement (e.g., recursive algorithm with off-by-one errors, or complex state machine). Run the full service with real LLM and real test suite. Verify final code passes all tests. Run the test 5+ times to ensure success is not a fluke—each run will have different LLM behavior and refinement paths, and all should eventually succeed (or all fail consistently if refinement is broken).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Single Agentic test (one LLM call) can't test refinement. Mocked Integration test defeats the purpose—mocks always succeed/fail predictably, hiding real refinement dynamics. Workflow level is essential.
